---
title: "Survey Weighting Comparison"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
  docx: default
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(survey)
library(dplyr)
library(gt)         # for HTML tables
library(kableExtra) # for PDF/Word tables

# Assumes 'survey' data frame and 'design' object are already in your workspace.
# If running standalone, load them here:
# survey <- readRDS("survey_with_weights.rds")
# design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weight, data = survey)
```

```{r build-comparison}
# Variables to summarize (must match column names in 'survey')
vars_comp <- c("age", "male", "white", "college", "democrat", "republican")

# Friendly labels for the table — same length and order as vars_comp
var_labels <- c(
  age        = "Age (years)",
  male       = "Male",
  white      = "White (non-Hispanic)",
  college    = "College graduate",
  democrat   = "Democrat",
  republican = "Republican"
)

# Build the comparison table
comparison <- data.frame(
  Variable   = var_labels[vars_comp],
  Unweighted = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) mean(survey[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)),
  Weighted   = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) {
                 as.numeric(svymean(as.formula(paste0("~", v)),
                                    design, na.rm = TRUE))
               }),
  row.names = NULL
) |>
  mutate(Difference = Weighted - Unweighted)
```

## Weighted vs. Unweighted Sample Means

The table below compares unweighted sample means to means after raking on age, race, gender, education, and party identification.

### Version 1: `gt` table (recommended for HTML)

```{r gt-table}
comparison |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Sample Composition Before and After Weighting",
    subtitle = "Raked to ACS 2023 and Pew NPORS benchmarks"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = c(Unweighted, Weighted, Difference), decimals = 3) |>
  cols_label(
    Variable   = "",
    Unweighted = "Unweighted",
    Weighted   = "Weighted",
    Difference = "Difference"
  ) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md("*Source: Author's survey (N = 1,500). Weights via iterative raking (anesrake).*")
  ) |>
  data_color(
    columns = Difference,
    fn = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("#d73027", "white", "#1a9850"),
      domain  = c(-0.2, 0.2)
    )
  )
```

### Version 2: `kable` table (works for PDF and Word)

```{r kable-table}
comparison |>
  mutate(across(c(Unweighted, Weighted, Difference), \(x) round(x, 3))) |>
  kbl(
    caption   = "Sample Composition Before and After Weighting",
    col.names = c("", "Unweighted", "Weighted", "Difference"),
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("l", "r", "r", "r")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    latex_options     = c("striped", "hold_position"),
    full_width        = FALSE
  ) |>
  footnote(
    general = "Weights computed via iterative raking to ACS 2023 and Pew NPORS targets.",
    general_title = "Note:",
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  )
```

## Optional: Add standard errors

```{r build-comparison-se}
comparison_se <- data.frame(
  Variable = var_labels[vars_comp],
  
  Unw_Mean = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) mean(survey[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)),
  Unw_SE   = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) {
               x <- survey[[v]][!is.na(survey[[v]])]
               sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
             }),
  
  Wt_Mean = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) {
              as.numeric(svymean(as.formula(paste0("~", v)),
                                 design, na.rm = TRUE))
            }),
  Wt_SE   = sapply(vars_comp, function(v) {
              SE(svymean(as.formula(paste0("~", v)),
                         design, na.rm = TRUE))
            }),
  row.names = NULL
) |>
  mutate(Difference = Wt_Mean - Unw_Mean)
```

```{r gt-table-se}
comparison_se |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Sample Means with Standard Errors",
    subtitle = "Unweighted and weighted estimates"
  ) |>
  fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 3) |>
  cols_label(
    Variable   = "",
    Unw_Mean   = "Mean",
    Unw_SE     = "SE",
    Wt_Mean    = "Mean",
    Wt_SE      = "SE",
    Difference = "Diff"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(label = "Unweighted", columns = c(Unw_Mean, Unw_SE)) |>
  tab_spanner(label = "Weighted",   columns = c(Wt_Mean, Wt_SE)) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  )
```
